location.office_time_required: 3 days per week on Mondays, Wednesdays, and Thursdays

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in stock plan administration
Partners with senior leadership from legal, finance, and people organizations
Comp range $121,440 - $151,800 base, consistent with a Senior IC
Reports to Director, Global Equity Programs

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience in stock plan administration, including with public companies on a global scale.
must_haves.qualifier: public companies on a global scale

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have experience effectively partnering with Total Rewards teams, with international equity plans (including M&A equity integration), and supporting Compensation Committee processes.
You operate like an owner. You are self-motivated, reliable, flexible, and can drive progress under tight deadlines.

You have exceptional analytical and conceptual thinking skills, have a strong track record of achievement and have a strong tendency toward cross-functional relationship building and a bias toward action.
You can distill complex concepts into simple, easy to understand communications.
Very strong Excel skills are required.
Deep knowledge of equity accounting and operating in international jurisdictions.
You are capable of identifying and resolving issues before they surface.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Knowledge of Workday, ADP, and the Schwab Equiview platform is a huge plus.
You have a strong desire to learn and teach, constantly seeking opportunities to develop team members, and be developed by team members.
Sense of humor, positive attitude, low ego, and a willingness to dive into new areas.

scope.surface: Global equity programs including RSUs, PSUs, ESPP, capital markets transactions, Section 16 filings, SOX compliance, M&A equity integration, and international equity plans
primary_metrics_inferred: Accuracy and timeliness of equity award processing
Employee education program reach and effectiveness
System automation efficiency on Schwab Equiview
core_responsibilities: Support administration of Lyft's global equity programs including processing all awards, grants, exercises, cancellations and the issuance of all new grants in the US and in foreign jurisdictions, including managing RSUs, PSUs, ESPP, capital markets transactions and other equity programs.
Continue to drive system automation on the Schwab Equiview platform across Workday and ADP.
Develop and deliver world class employee education programs for new and continuing employees with a focus on mechanics and overall financial wellness.
Support all aspects of SOX compliance and continuously work to build efficiencies around execution and documentation of key controls.
Engage with senior leadership from the legal, finance, and people organizations to design future equity programs and policies.
Coordinate closely with the payroll team to ensure proper withholding of tax from equity proceeds.
Define domestic and international integration strategies (deal specific) for equity, support M&A equity integration process.
Assist with D&O Questionnaires and Proxy disclosure regarding director and executive officer stock ownership.
Support the 10b5-1 program for executives and insiders across two brokers.
Support the timely filing of Section 16 reports (Forms 3, 4 and 5) with the SEC.

compensation.equity_form: potential equity offering mentioned as not included in base range

green_flags: Clear and detailed list of responsibilities — role scope is well-defined
Hybrid schedule with specific days named (Mon/Wed/Thu) — predictable cadence
Reports directly to Director, Global Equity Programs — clear reporting line
Strong benefits package including 18 weeks paid parental leave and 401(k) match
Flexibility to work from anywhere for up to 4 weeks per year
red_flags: Seattle location only listed for comp range — other office locations and pay bands not disclosed
Hybrid role requiring 3 days/week in-office eliminates fully remote candidates
JD does not specify if people management is expected, though seniority signals suggest IC role — mild ambiguity

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from comp range, years required, and reporting structure)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; no management language in JD)
scope.business_model (inferred from Lyft's known rideshare product)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated in JD)
location.work_auth (inferred as open; no restrictions stated)
ambiguities: JD states 'The expected base pay range for this position in the Seattle area is...' implying role may exist in other locations, but no other cities are listed for this role — treated as Seattle only for cities_listed.

CEP certification: JD says 'preferred' but also says 'Very strong Excel skills are required' and 'Knowledge of Workday, ADP, and the Schwab Equiview platform is a huge plus' in the same bullet — classified CEP as soft (preferred) and Excel as hard (required), Workday/ADP/Schwab as soft (huge plus).
